Abecean Longfin.description: Abecean Longfins are fish that live in bodies of water all over Skyrim.

Adamantium Ore.description: Adamantium (also called Adamantine) is a rare and ageless metal used for making valuable armor and weapons. It is typically seen in a lustrous silvery color dubbed "Adamantine Silver", though adamantium equipment can also have a stone blue hue or a dark grey coloration. The ore is very heavy and can be used in alchemy. Adamantium armor can appear as either a high-quality heavy weight armor or medium weight armor. It is above the quality of Mithril or Orichalcum, but is inferior to Stalhrim and Ebony. The armor has a high potential for enchantment. Adamantium weapons can harm magical creatures that resist normal weapons.

Alkanet Flower.description: Alkanet Flower is a fairly common flower in the West Weald region of Cyrodiil. The flowers are usually found in groups of two to five and normally grow near rocks.

Aloe Vera Leaves.description: Aloe Vera Leaves come from the Aloe Vera plant and are well-known for their healing properties. Aloe can prevent bleeding as well as lessen the effects of pox.

Amber.description: Amber is a colorful resin formed from hardened sap. It is especially common in the Shivering Isles, and blacksmiths there often craft amber weapons and armor.

Ambrosia.description: The ingredient Ambrosia is one of two ingredients that comes from the plant Mana Bloom. Mana Blooms grow almost exclusively on the plane Gaiar Alata and therefore Ambrosia is quite a rare ingredient.

Ampoule Pod.description: Ampoule Pods are harvested from draggle-tail plants, commonly found in the Bitter Coast region of Vvardenfell.

Antlers.description: The antlers of deer and elk can be used in alchemy, though the effects differ depending on size.

Apple.description: Apples are a common fruit encountered throughout Tamriel.

Arrowroot.description: The ingredient Arrowroot comes from the plant Arrowroot Plant, which grows in the Gold Coast and Nibenay Valley regions of Cyrodiil.

Ash Salt.description: Ash Salt is a highly magical substance made up of hard grey crystals formed from the ash deposited by ash storms. Ash Salt is often collected from the remains of creatures like the ash slave, the ash ghoul, and ash zombie.

Ashen Grass Pod.description: Ashen Grass Pods is an ingredient harvested from Spiky Grass plants unique to Solstheim. The plant looks similar to the grass pod found in mainland Skyrim, only this type is grey in color and has different alchemical properties.

Ashen Remains.description: Ashen Remains are the remnants of bones burned using the Crematorium in Ebrocca, in the Shivering Isles.

Aster Bloom Core.description: Aster Bloom Cores can be harvested from Aster Bloom plants, which are only found in the Mania region of the Shivering Isles.

Beetle Scuttle.description: Beetle Scuttle is a cheese-like, greasy substance made from the flesh of beetle-like creatures, including Assassin Beetles, Thunderbugs, and Shalks.

Bergamot Seeds.description: Bergamot Seeds comes from the plant Bergamot, most commonly found in the Great Forest region of Cyrodiil.

Bittergreen Petals.description: Bittergreen is a red flowering plant with modest magical properties which grow abundantly in the West Gash region, and in lesser quantities in the Ashlands and Red Mountain regions of Vvardenfell.

Blackberry.description: Blackberries come from the plant Blackberry Bush, commonly found growing in the West Weald region of Cyrodiil.

Blessed Thistle.description: Blessed Thistle is a plant with many spade-shaped green leaves growing from a central stem. The plant is found all across Tamriel.

Blister Pod Cap.description: Blister Pod Caps comes from the plant Blister Pod which grows exclusively in the Dementia region of the Shivering Isles.

Blisterwort.description: Blisterwort is a variety of fungus that is commonly found growing in caves.

Bloat.description: Bloat is a thick, pulpy-white tuber of the bloatspore plant. It grows in dark, damp locations.

Bloodgrass.description: Bloodgrass comes from the plant Blood Grass, which is a plant native to the Daedric Plane of Mehrunes Dagon, The Deadlands. It sometimes grows near Oblivion Gates that leads to the Deadlands.

Blue Entoloma.description: Blue Entoloma comes from the Entoloma mushroom, a vibrant blue fungi. It can be found growing all across Tamriel.

Bog Beacon Asco Cap.description: Bog Beacon Asco Caps comes from the plant Bog Beacon. The plant is most commonly found in the Blackwood region of Cyrodiil.

Bone Marrow.description: Bone Marrow, sometimes called Shambles Marrow, is collected from dead Shambles.

Bone Meal.description: Bonemeal is a finely ground powder made from bone. It possesses modest magical properties and can be harvested from skeletons and liches.

Bugloss.description: Bugloss is a large plant with purple-blue flowers that grows all across Tamriel.

Bungler's Bane.description: Bungler's Bane is a mottled brown-and-orange shelf fungus collected from the trunks of trees and occasionally on cave walls. Commonly found in the Bitter Coast region of Vvardenfell.

Cairn Bolete Cap.description: Cairn Bolete Caps comes from the Cairn Bolete fungus. Cairn Bolete is most commonly found in caves but can occasionally be found in the wilderness.

Canis Root.description: Canis Root is harvested from small, gnarled bushes. Most commonly found in birch woods near rock formations or at the base of cliffs.

Chaurus Eggs.description: Chaurus Eggs are harvested from Chaurus egg sacs. The eggs and sacs emit a pale blue bioluminescence.

Chokeberry.description: Chokeberries comes from the Chokeberry Vines. They are prized by assassins for being deadly poisonous if consumed.

Chokeweed.description: Chokeweed is a tough shrub growing in the rocky highlands of the West Gash region of Vvardenfell.

Cinnabar Polypore Red Cap.description: Cinnabar Polypore Red Caps comes from the fungus Cinnabar Polypore, which is a somewhat hard-to-find mushroom that primarily grows on the sides of trees in the West Weald region of Cyrodiil.

Clam Gall.description: Clam Gall is an ingredient that can be found in Giant Clams on the beaches of Summerset Isle.

Clannfear Claws.description: Clannfear Claws is primarily collected from dead Clannfears. Claws can only be collected from fully grown Clannfears, not from Clannfear Runts.

Clouded Funnel Cap.description: Clouded Funnel Caps comes from the fungus Clouded Funnel Cap, which is a mushroom commonly found in high altitude regions and in the Blackwood region of Cyrodiil.

Coda Flower.description: The coda flower is the fruiting body collected from the large primitive draggle-tail plant which grows in shallow swamp-water. They are most commonly found in the Bitter Coast region of Vvardenfell. Coda Flowers have an interesting property in that they glow in the dark. The plants actually emit light that illuminates things around them.

Columbine.description: Columbine is a small plant with many drooping pink flowers that can be found all across Tamriel. It is a prized ingredient for many potions due to its effects.

Comberry.description: Comberries comes from the Comberry Plant, a bush that produces a bitter berry, best known as the basis of comberry brandy, a rough but potent alcoholic beverage of Morrowind. Comberry is commonly grown in the Ascadian Isles region of Vvardenfell

Congealed Putrescence.description: Congealed Putrescence comes from the fungus Putrid Gigantea, which is native to the Dementia region of the Shivering Isles.

Corkbulb Root.description: Corkbulb Roots comes from Corkbulb Trees which are grown for their tough, fibrous roots which have modest magical properties and can also be used to fashion arrows and bolts. It is rarely seen in the wild.. On Vvardenfell, corkbulb root is used in place of wood, since trees won't grow in the harsh environment. Corkbulb grows best in the Ascadian Isles.

Corn.description: Corn is a common staple food harvested from Corn Stalks.

Corn Flower.description: The Corn Flower is a small plant with blue petals that grow all across Tamriel.

Corprus Weepings.description: Corprus weepings are hardened secretions that fall from the fleshy sores of corprus victims.

Crab Meat.description: Crab Meat comes from Mudcrabs and is used as both food and in alchemy due to its modest magical properties.

Creep Cluster.description: Creep Clusters are masses of strange orange-brown roots found growing over rocks in the sulfurous region of Eastmarch in Skyrim.

Crimson Nirnroot.description: Crimson Nirnroot is a special variant of nirnroot that can be found exclusively in Blackreach. Similar to regular nirnroot, the crimson variety makes a strange humming sound.

Cyrodilic Spadetail.description: Cyrodilic Spadetails are fish that can be caught in ponds, lakes, and other bodies of water.

Daedra Heart.description: A Daedra Heart (or aortas daedrae) is an ingredient that can be harvested from slain Daedra, primarily Dremora. In older times, they could be found on many other varieties of Daedra, including Clannfears, Daedroths, Golden Saints, Hungers, Ogrims, and Xivilai. These beings, to the extent that they can still be encountered, now produce other ingredients.  But Dremora have consistently been a reliable source for Daedra Hearts.

Daedra Silk.description: Daedra Silk is produced by Spider Daedra, and can be harvested from their corpses.

Daedra Skin.description: Daedra Skin is a very rare ingredient.

Daedra Venin.description: Daedra Venin is produced by Spider Daedra, and can be extracted from their corpses.

Daedroth Teeth.description: Daedroth Teeth are harvested from the deadly Daedroth, a rare Daedra with crocodilian features.

Deathbell.description: Deathbells are flowers that grow in Skyrim and Cyrodiil, useful in making poisons.

Diamond.description: Diamonds are mined from volcanic regions such as Vvardenfell in Morrowind, and can also be found in mineral deposits in Skyrim.  They are also commonly found in the ruins of the Dwemer, who presumably had industrial uses for them.

Dragon's Tongue.description: Dragon's Tongue flowers grow in Cyrodiil and Skyrim, and are valued for their fire resistance abilities.

Dragonthorn.description: Dragonthorn flowers grow in all of Tamriel and are valued for stamina restoration abilities. The flowers won't grow in saltwater.

Dreugh Wax.description: Dreugh Wax is found on Dreugh, both the sea-bound variety seen around Vvardenfell and their land-based cousins in Cyrodiil. Dreugh which consume Ash Basil are known to produce a more durable wax.

Drothweed.description: Drothweed is a substance that has the poisonous properties of greatly decreasing strength while also draining speed and agility. It can be created by combining palm, bamboo, and black rose together.

Dry Juniper Leaf.description: Dry Juniper Leaf is a green leaf that comes from the Juniper tree. It's used in medicines and certain Ash'abah rituals.

Dryad Saddle Polypore Cap.description: Dryad Saddle Polypore Caps are a rare shelf fungus found on stone walls and caves in Cyrodiil.

Durzog Meat.description: Durzog Meat is the meat of the Durzog, a dog-like reptilian creature found both domesticated and wild in the Deshaan region of Morrowind.

Dwarven Oil.description: Dwarven Oil is extracted from Dwarven automatons, found in Dwemer ruins in Skyrim.  Similar machines found around Vvardenfell were once used as a source of Scrap Metal.

Ectoplasm.description: Ectoplasm is a filmy residue with modest magical properties that remains after a ghost, or spirit has been banished from the mortal plane. Garments made of ectoplasm are lightweight, frigid, and useful for keeping smiths from suffering heat exhaustion.

Elf Cup Cap.description: Elf Cup Caps comes from the plant Elf Cup, which is a mushroom found in the West Weald region of Cyrodiil.

Elytra Ichor.description: Elytra Ichor is harvested from dead elytra which are native to the Shivering Isles.

Emerald.description: Emeralds are a rare transparent green gem with modest magical properties.

Emetic Russula.description: Emetic Russula is a red fungus that can be found all across Tamriel.

Emperor Parasol Moss.description: Emperor Parasol Moss can often be found hanging from Emperor Parasol mushrooms native to Morrowind. Moss hanging from the Emperor Parasols on Solstheim is known to be used in alchemy.

Fennel Seeds.description: Fennel Seeds comes from the plant Fennel, which is most commonly found in the northern part of the Gold Coast region of Cyrodiil.

Fire Petal.description: Fire Petals are collected from the fire fern plant that grows in the Red Mountain, and Azura's Coast regions of Vvardenfell.

Fire Salts.description: Fire salts are a crystalline compound that may be collected from the remains of fire atronachs that have been banished from the mortal plane.

Flame Stalk.description: Flame Stalks comes from the plant of the same name that exclusively grows in the Mania region of the Shivering Isles.

Flax Seeds.description: Flax Seeds comes from the plant Flax, which can be found all across Tamriel. There are three varieties of the plant, one with yellow flowers, one with red flowers, and one with blue flowers.

Foxglove Nectar.description: Foxglove Nectar comes from the plant Foxglove, which is very common in the Nibenay Valley region of Cyrodiil.

Frost Salts.description: Frost Salts are the crystalline compound that may be collected from the remains of frost atronachs that have been banished from the mortal plane. Refined Frost Salts are samples prepared by mages possessing even greater purity.

Fungus Stalk.description: Fungus Stalks comes from the plant of the same name, which are native to the Dementia region of the Shivering Isles.

Ghoul Heart.description: Ghoul Hearts are incredibly rare heart tissues collected from the remains of ash ghouls.

Giant Lichen.description: Giant Lichen is harvested from a fungus of the same name which is found in the marshes of Hjaalmarch in the province of Skyrim.

Giant's Toe.description: Giant's Toes are gathered from slain Giants. The toes of a Giant have extremelly potent benefits both in alchemy and enchanting.

Gingko Leaf.description: Gingko Leaves are harvested from the eponymous Gingko Trees that are found throughout the provinces of Cyrodiil, Summerset Isle, as well as close to the rivers and lakes of Hammerfell.

Ginseng.description: Ginseng can be harvested from the Ginseng Plant, which can be found all across Tamriel. There are two varieties of the plant, one with yellow flowers and one with red flowers.

Gleamblossom.description: Gleamblossom is harvested from the plant of the same name, a purple flower found mainly within the Forgotten Vale in Skyrim. There have also been rare samples found in Valenwood caves.

Glow Dust.description: Glow Dust are harvested from wisps, wispmothers, and will-o-the-wisps. It has a strange consistency, a little like the web of Frostbite spiders. There are two varieties of glow dust——Vital Glow Dust, which is the remains of freshly departed Wispmothers, and Faint Glow Dust, which is the faded remains of a wisp, or a wispmother of inconsiderable power.

Glowing Mushroom.description: Glowing Mushrooms are a bioluminescent fungus that grows on the wall of caves. It is found abundantly in Skyrim, but can also be found elsewhere.

Gnarl Bark.description: Gnarl Bark is harvested from Gnarls that inhabit the Shivering Isles, as well as from Spriggans corrupted by the influence of Sheogorath's realm.

Gold Kanet.description: Gold Kanet is a flowering plant commonly found in the Ascadian Isles, and Azura's Coast regions of Vvardenfell.

Golden Sedge Flowers.description: Golden Sedge Flowers are a yellow flower that can only be found in the city of Mournhold in Morrowind.

Grahl Eyeball.description: Grahl Eyeballs are a rare ingredient that can be extracted from slain Grahls, which are often found in the ice caves of Solstheim. Grahls are noted for possessing a third eye. Grahl eyeballs are sometimes mummified and repurposed as tough, leathery pouches for carrying small items.

Grass Pod.description: Grass Pods are harvested from Spiky Grass plants that grow along Skyrim's northern coasts and along the rivers around Morthal.

Gravedust.description: Gravedust is spirit-affinitive dust taken from remains buried in consecrated ground.

Green Lichen.description: Green lichen is a rare and hardy primitive plant with modest magical properties that grows in the West Gash region of Vvardenfell.

Green Stain Cup Cap.description: Green Stain Cup Caps comes from the plant Green Stain Cup, which is a very common mushroom in the Blackwood region, but can also be found in smaller quantities in the Great Forest and Nibenay Valley regions of Cyrodiil.

Green Stain Shelf Cap.description: Green Stain Shelf Cap is a rare mushroom that comes from the plant LoreGreen Stain Shelf|Green Stain Shelf, easily mistaken for the abundant Green Stain Cup mushroom. Green Stain Shelf mushrooms tend to grow on the sides of trees and rocks, whereas the Cup variety tends to grow on the ground.

Hackle-Lo Leaf.description: Hackle-lo leaf is an edible succulent leaf native to the Grazelands region of Vvardenfell. It's prized both for its taste and its restorative powers.

Hanging Moss.description: Hanging Moss is a plant found growing off rocky ledges, trees, and the ceilings of caves.

Harrada.description: Harrada comes from the Harrada Root plant, which is native to Mehrunes Dagon's realm of Oblivion, the Deadlands. Normally Harrada Root plants are not found in Tamriel, however they take root outside of certain Oblivion Gates that lead to the Deadlands.

Heartwood.description: Heartwood is a rare ingredient that can be harvested from Spriggans.

Heather.description: Heather is a low evergreen shrub native to the Ascadian Isles region of Vvardenfell. It's known for its small, pinkish-purple flowers and modest magical properties.

Holly Berries.description: Holly Berries are a common ingredient that grows in bushes all across Solstheim.

Hound Meat.description: Hound meat or nix-hound meat is the flesh of the nix-hound. The meat is sweet and tender, and has several magical properties.

Hunger Tongue.description: Hunger Tongues are harvested from Hungers, a ferocious Daedra. The tongues can be used by alchemists as an ingredient in potions to cure diseases and aid those who are afflicted by poison.

Hydnum Azure Giant Spore.description: Hydnum Azure Giant Spores comes from the mushroom Hydnum Azure, which is native to the Mania region of the Shivering Isles.

Imp Stool.description: Imp stools are squat, orange-capped, yellow, or golden-brown mushrooms that grow in caves, outside in the shade of trees or rocks, and on the bodies of Shroom Beetles.

References.description: Note The following references are considered to be unofficial sources. They are included to round off this article and may not be authoritative or conclusive.

Juniper Berries.description: Juniper Berries comes from the Juniper tree. It's prized for both its alchemical and flavorful propereties. Nords often use Juniper Berries to add extra flavor to mead.

Kwama Cuttle.description: Kwama cuttle is a tough, waxy substance that comes from the beak of kwama creatures. Kwama cuttle has several magical properties.

Kwama Egg.description: Kwama eggs are a rich, nutritious foodstuff with several magical properties. Popular throughout the Empire, kwama eggs stay fresh for a long time, and kwama mining is a profitable industry on Vvardenfell.

Magebane.description: Magebane is a substance that has the poisonous properties of greatly decreasing magicka while also draining willpower. It can be created by combining ghost essence, Werewolf's blood, and rubies together.

Moonseed.description: Moonseed is a substance that has the poisonous properties of draining health. It can be created by combining Gingko leaves green leaves, Werewolf's blood, and black poppy together.

Mort Flesh.description: Mort Flesh refers to the myriad of different appendages that have been harvested from the reanimated dead—Mort fingers, mort tongues, and mort hearts. Careful handling is important, as mort flesh carries several potentially fatal diseases such as Ataxia, Helljoint, and Witbane. It has several uses in both alchemy and enchanting. It has a sweet sickly odor.

Muck.description: Muck is the damp, fibrous slime from crushed muckspunge plants that grow in the West Gash, and Azura's Coast regions of Vvardenfell. Unless properly prepared, it is mildly toxic.

Mummy wrappings.description: Mummy wrappings are the cloth wrappings which were used in the preservation of a mummified corpse. Some alchemy recipes call for freshly harvested wrappings from a specific mummy.

Nux Vomica.description: Nux Vomica is a substance that has the poisonous properties of draining health.

Phyrric Acid.description: Phyrric Acid sic is a substance that has the poisonous properties of greatly draining health.  It can be created by combining Daedra hearts, Orc's blood, dragon scales, and red poppy together.

Powdered Mother of Pearl.description: Powdered Mother of Pearl is an ingredient that can be found in Giant Clams on the beaches of Summerset Isle.

Scalon Fin.description: Scalon fins are harvested from Scalons for their useful alchemical properties. Scalons are native to the Shivering Isles, but by E  samples of Scalon fin could be found circulating on Tamriel as a rare alchemy ingredient.

Scamp Skin.description: Scamp skin can be removed from Scamps while it is still bound on the mortal plane. It is a rare ingredient and alchemists have trouble keeping the substance in stock. It is used for straining in alchemy and cooking.UOL

Sload Soap.description: Sload Soap is a waxy substance made from the immature non-sentient forms of the Sload.

Thyrwort.description: Thyrwort is a substance that has the poisonous properties of draining willpower and personality. It can be created by combining brass, basilisk's eye, small teeth, and mercury together.

Vampire Dust.description: Vampire Dust is the ash collected from deceased vampires for its alchemical properties. It is dangerous to collect, and is thus highly valued and expensive. Its alchemical effects vary as much as the environments from which the dust is collected. For example, Vampire Dust collected from vampires in Vvardenfell is the only variety known to be able transmit the disease, but because it's the only ingredient capable of it no potion can be made which offers this effect. Dust collected from vampires in Cyrodiil and Skyrim both in turn carry a resistance to diseases and invisibility, both effects carried from their vampiric source.

Violet Coprinus.description: Violet Coprinus is a slender-stemmed, bluish-purple, or sometimes light-green-capped mushroom found all across Tamriel.

Viper's Bugloss Leaves.description: Viper's Bugloss Leaves comes from the plant Viper's Bugloss, which grows abundantly in the Great Forest region of Cyrodiil.

Void Essence.description: Void Essence is a type of red gemstone harvested from Flesh Atronach corpses. It should not be confused with the inky substance known as Void Essence, which can be harvested from Shades and other dark spirits associated with the Void but cannot be used for alchemy.

Void Salts.description: Void Salts are rare crystalline precipitates collected from the remains of slain Storm Atronachs and Winged Twilights.

Wickwheat.description: Wickwheat is a wild Ashland grain with several magical properties that grows in the Grazelands region of Vvardenfell.

Willow Anther.description: Willow anther is the powdery residue from the pollen-bearing parts of the willow flower that grows in the Ascadian Isles. and Azura's Coast regions of Vvardenfell.
